decision_basis: Beech 01 and 02 are tall but sparse, top-heavy, and visually thin at river distance.
Beech 03 is a sparse juvenile structure that nearly disappears in the 60 m view.
Beech 04 has greater local leaf mass but reads as a dark shrub-like silhouette and also loses useful canopy character at 60 m.
The installed twig materials render excessively dark under the fixed Futaleufu review photometry and do not provide stable layered crown breakup.
Fagus sylvatica is a non-native structure analog and cannot establish Patagonian or Nothofagus ecology approval.

review_contract.capture_redistribution: not approved; hashes and reproducible paths only are retained in the open-source repository

variants.finding: Tall sparse vertical silhouette with a top-heavy crown, large open gaps, dark twig masses, and inadequate continuous canopy at 60 m.
Tall narrow trunk and sparse upper crown remain visually thin from both turntable angles and fail to build dense rainforest slope mass at 60 m.
Small juvenile structure has only 201 twig instances, an open triangular crown, and almost no useful silhouette at the 60 m proxy distance.
The locally denser crown remains short, dark, and shrub-like; oversized twig masses collapse into a nearly black silhouette and lose identity at 60 m.

next_asset_requirements: Rights-clear native Patagonian or ecology-reviewed Nothofagus-family species identity.
Several adult and intermediate age variants with broad, layered, distance-stable crowns.
Leaf and branch materials proven under Metal, Nanite, two-sided foliage, and fixed river-camera photometry.
Forest-band ecology that combines canopy, subcanopy, saplings, ferns, deadwood, and edge response without obvious repetition.
Measured desktop and VR performance before any production promotion.
